items.title: Creatine doesn't just build muscle. It may also help fight cancer
items.summary: Scientists have discovered that creatine may strengthen one of the immune system's most important cancer-fighting pathways by energizing dendritic cells that activate killer T cells. items.title: Can't stick to a diet? Intermittent fasting may be easier than counting calories for weight loss
items.summary: Intermittent fasting helped people lose as much weight as calorie restriction, but without the same feeling of constantly controlling their food intake. Researchers say that difference could make fasting a more sustainable option for people who struggle with traditional diets.

items.title: Takeaway meals contain more salt than advertised, study finds
items.summary: Almost half of the takeaway meals tested contained more salt than advertised, with some dishes delivering nearly twice the recommended daily limit in a single serving. Surprisingly, classic fish and chips ranked among the lowest-salt options, while pasta, pizza, and curries were often the saltiest.
